calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Corman, via ZOOM, for Cecile Soliai, Administrator; Constance Figuers, via ZOOM, for Probate Advance, LLC, Assignee; Kina Casey, via ZOOM, Heir; Yvette Casey, via ZOOM, Heir.

The court begins by advising Ms. Corman that the court typically requires two (2) year Accountings. The court also advises counsel that it is waiving any defect in notice as well as the required two-year accounting and moving forward, counsel should note to include dates with schedules. The court asks Ms. Figeurs if she has anything to add. Ms. Figuers does not. Ms. Corman then confirms with the court that if this petition is granted, she will be required to submit an ex parte application to withdraw funds from a blocked account. The court suggests that once the application is filed, a copy of the filed application and proposed order be dropped-off to the court for quicker processing, instead of sending via U.S. mail.

The Sixth Amended Final Account, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Bank Statements For Bank Of America And Chase Bank
Off Calendar Mar-24-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Corman appeared by zoom for Cecile Soliai (Administrator).

The Court noted that it received the bank statements and that the seller's statement is in the e-file queue. Ms. Corman stated that Ms. Soliai relocated to Texas at the beginning of the pandemic and that it was supposed to be temporary, but she is permanently there. The Court noted that the money is in banks other than what was indicated on the sale order and that the accounts are in El Paso, Texas and in Las Cruces, New Mexico. Ms. Corman stated that she attempted to place the money in those banks with Ms. Soliai being in Texas, but the banks wouldn't allow it and that US Bank does not have a location in Texas. The Court stated that there is an issue with the accounts being in other States and that it is glad Counsel provided the receipts because they kind of show that the banks are acknowledging jurisdiction. Ms. Corman stated that there is an outstanding mortgage and an unknown judgment in a form of a lien against the house, and that it will show on the closing statement. Status on filing of the bank statements and status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts are off calendar. Status on filing of the seller's statement is set for April 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For The Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Continued To Mar-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Corman appeared by zoom for Cecile Solian (Petitioner).

Ms. Corman stated that they have been having quite an issue with the banks legal department in getting signed receipts for the blocked accounts, that they got a receipt for the account at US Bank, that Bank of America and Chase Bank won't take her calls, and that she is told that they need to communicate with the Administrator and then the Administrator is told that they need to communicate with the Attorney. Ms. Corman also stated that they are now working with people in the local branches, that the accounts were established when they sold the house, that she is not sure why there is an issue with the legal departments and that she has bank statements showing that they are blocked accounts. The Court instructed Ms. Corman to file the receipt from US Bank and the statements from Bank of America and Chase Bank. Ms. Corman stated that she will be filing an ex parte petition for withdrawal of the funds from the blocked accounts. Ms. Corman is to advise the assigned Probate Examiner when she files the ex parte petition. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts is continued to March 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the bank statements is also set for the same date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Report Of Sale And Petition For Order Confirming Sale Of 1653 Oakdale Ave, San Francisco 94124)
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Corman appeared in Court for and with Cecile Soliai (Petitioner) by zoom.
Alexander Mulder (Listing Agent) appeared in Court in Propria Persona.
Thomas Nguyen (Buyer's Agent) appeared by zoom for and with Phuong Nguyen (buyer) by zoom telephone.

This matter was called and passed more than once due to audio issues on the Buyers end and due to the Buyer disconnecting prior to the matter being recalled. The Court stated that it generally sees more terms of the sale and noted that the petition contained no terms of the sale. Ms. Corman stated that it was an inadvertent mistake and provided a copy of the sales contract in open Court. The Court noted that the Buyer was not given notice and questions whether Phuong Nguyen saw the terms in the contract. Phuong Nguyen stated that he waives notice of this hearing, that he saw the terms in the contract and is familiar with the terms of the counter offer, and that he agrees to those terms. Ms. Corman informed the Court that there is an issue with her Client qualifying for a bond for the full amount and requested to have the funds placed in a blocked account. There being no over bids, the sale was confirmed at $925,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the contract and the counter offer. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (order for the same signed in open Court). The Court informed Ms. Corman that the proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into multiple blocked accounts for insured amounts in various/different financial institutions. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts is set for February 10, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate that is currently set for January 11, 2021 is continued to February 10, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
